entries.gameplayFunction: Primary dynastic seat and settlement anchor.
entries.visualRole: Highest-value civic and command building read.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Strong crown shape, clear main entry, immediate seat-of-power read.
entries.colorNotes: House banners, trims, and command cloth only.
entries.scaleNotes: Largest civic mass before grand structures.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Must embody House identity and governance logic.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs LOD, damage-state swaps, and keep-view references.

entries.gameplayFunction: Population cap growth and settlement prosperity read.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Small-to-medium dwellings, roof variation, density logic.
entries.materialNotes: Thatch, timber, tile, plaster, and regional stone as appropriate.
entries.colorNotes: Minimal House cues except civic trim.
entries.scaleNotes: Must read as repeatable settlement pieces.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Show prosperity and maintenance differences clearly.
entries.unityImportNotes: Likely modular packs by House and settlement class.

entries.gameplayFunction: Food production and agrarian ground read.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Field plots, small sheds, fences, water logic.
entries.materialNotes: Timber, rope, earth, crop rows, tool racks.
entries.colorNotes: Low House color; land and crop state carry read.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Read productive, not decorative village filler.

entries.gameplayFunction: Water infrastructure and field-army sustainment anchor.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Immediate draw-bucket and masonry ring read.
entries.materialNotes: Stone, timber frame, rope, bucket, troughs.
entries.scaleNotes: Small structure with strong vertical read.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Must read as water infrastructure, not generic prop.
entries.unityImportNotes: Useful for hydration radius debug visualization later.

entries.gameplayFunction: Food and goods storage plus prosperity read.
entries.visualRole: Resource security and settlement scale marker.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Raised or heavy storage mass, venting, bins, wagon access.
entries.materialNotes: Timber, plaster, stone base, sack and crate dressing.
entries.colorNotes: House trim minimal; grain and civic logic matter more.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Show believable loading and storage access.
entries.unityImportNotes: May split into food and mixed-resource families later.

entries.silhouetteNotes: Distinct extraction shapes by resource type.
entries.materialNotes: Timber stacks, cut stone, mine frames, smelting traces.
entries.scaleNotes: Medium footprint with strong adjacent ground treatment.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Each resource family must read instantly from silhouette and ground wear.
entries.unityImportNotes: Keep mine, quarry, and lumber packs separable for gameplay hooks.

entries.gameplayFunction: Arms production and iron-processing civic-military lane.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Chimney, vent, hammer line, furnace read.
entries.materialNotes: Stone hearth, iron hardware, charcoal residue, timber shelter.
entries.colorNotes: Warm internal light with muted exterior palette.
entries.scaleNotes: Medium building with recognizable roof and stack shape.
entries.unityImportNotes: Could share fire VFX hooks with Ironmark variants.

entries.silhouetteNotes: Drill yard, weapon rack, banner, training structure read.
entries.materialNotes: Timber, stone base, iron fittings, yard clutter kept disciplined.
entries.colorNotes: House banner and shield paint only.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Must read military without becoming fortress or stable.
entries.unityImportNotes: Should expose unit spawn point and drill-yard area.

entries.silhouetteNotes: Targets, range frame, firing line shelter, arrow storage.
entries.materialNotes: Timber, thatch or tile, target cloth, quiver racks.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Clearly different from barracks and stable.
entries.unityImportNotes: May start as concept-only until gameplay seat lands.

entries.silhouetteNotes: Wide roof, stall rhythm, paddock or tack cues.
entries.materialNotes: Timber, rope, leather tack, hay storage.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Read horse infrastructure instantly at RTS height.
entries.unityImportNotes: Need horse spawn and tack-prop variants.

entries.silhouetteNotes: Crane arms, heavy beams, wheel stock, engine frame parts.
entries.materialNotes: Timber, iron braces, rope, stone pads.
entries.colorNotes: Low House color, production materials dominate.
entries.scaleNotes: Large workshop footprint with exterior production clutter kept controlled.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Should read serious engineering, not generic barn.
entries.unityImportNotes: Supports ram, tower, trebuchet, ballista, mantlet queues.

entries.gameplayFunction: Forward logistics anchor and field-water support seat.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Tent, stacked crates, casks, cookfire, hitching zone.
entries.colorNotes: House cloth marks on covers and flags.
entries.scaleNotes: Medium footprint, open and readable from above.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Must read as logistics, not civilian market tents.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs wagon-link readability and raid-damage swaps.

entries.assetName: Walls / Gates / Towers / Keep Defense Family
entries.gameplayFunction: Fortification lane and settlement defense readability.
entries.visualRole: Most important structural combat read in the game.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Wall thickness, gate mass, tower crown, keep core all distinct.
entries.materialNotes: Stone, timber internals, iron gate hardware, defensive dressings.
entries.colorNotes: House banners and shield boards only.
entries.scaleNotes: Must read at both tactical and theatre scales.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Fortified and neglected seats must feel materially different.
entries.unityImportNotes: Needs breach sockets, damage states, banner attach points.

entries.gameplayFunction: Trade, civic prosperity, and route logic seat.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Stalls, awnings, counters, central exchange mass.
entries.scaleNotes: Open civic zone rather than single tall mass.
entries.unityImportNotes: Could anchor future trade and neutral-city scenes.

entries.gameplayFunction: Shrine, temple, sanctuary, and covenant-presence lane.
entries.visualRole: Faith identity without erasing House architecture.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Distinct covenant read through structure and ritual elements.
entries.materialNotes: Covenant-specific overlays on House and terrain base.
entries.colorNotes: Faith colors are secondary overlays, not total replacements.
entries.scaleNotes: Ranges from field shrine to major sanctuary.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Covenants must remain materially grounded and structurally plausible.
entries.unityImportNotes: Best handled as modular House base plus covenant kits.

entries.gameplayFunction: Reserved lane for House-specific landmark buildings.
entries.visualRole: Civic and dynastic asymmetry beyond the shared roster.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Use House identity pack until gameplay canon locks.
entries.colorNotes: House trim and heraldic logic strongest here.
entries.scaleNotes: Landmark structures larger than standard civic buildings.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Do not invent gameplay effect; reserve visual lane only.
entries.unityImportNotes: Keep in staging until House landmark canon settles.

entries.gameplayFunction: Endgame seats, declarations, or civilizational landmarks.
entries.visualRole: Scale escalation and realm maturity read.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Should dominate settlement skyline without losing purpose clarity.
entries.materialNotes: High-fidelity stone, metal, and banner discipline.
entries.colorNotes: House and faith overlays carefully balanced.
entries.scaleNotes: Largest built masses in the game.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Reserve for later once late-game structure canon is assembled.
entries.unityImportNotes: Likely separate streaming or LOD treatment.

entries.assetName: Ruins / Neutral Settlement / Trade Hub Family
entries.gameplayFunction: Neutral world logic, salvage, and historical depth.
entries.silhouetteNotes: Ruins must read abandoned or reclaimed; neutral towns must read governable.
entries.materialNotes: Age-worn stone, repaired timber, market and salvage traces.
entries.colorNotes: Neutral palette with limited civic markings.
entries.scaleNotes: Varies from fragments to full trade hubs.
entries.generationPromptNotes: Trade hub should feel world-important, not fantasy capital glamor.
entries.unityImportNotes: Supports neutral city, frost ruin, and trade-hub future lanes.
